parsed_review.summary: CP00-855 is a descriptor-only RP28 Marketplace And Custom AI Apps API/UI foundation pack (150 units, RP28.P03.M06.S13-RP28.P04.M03.S20, risk C). Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is supported and not overstated. (1) Binding, contract JSON, manifest, baseline, and README are mutually consistent and aligned with the live closeout-pack-plan cursor — CP00-855 is the first/live-open pack with matching range, unit_count, and source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65. (2) The deliverable distribution {test:13,hermes_evidence:7,claude_review:11,implementation:53,contract:17,security_audit:16,ui:30,fixture:3} and micro-title distribution match the prompt baseline exactly; I independently re-derived the case-set row math (8+22+20+20+10+10+20+20+20 = 150) from API_ROWS (22) and UI_ROWS (20). (3) Every case-set row (createRow) carries descriptor_only=true plus a comprehensive no-runtime/no-write/no-leak attestation, and validateMarketplaceCp855ApiUiFoundationDescriptor enforces those flags, blocks runtime/write/secret/real-data, and verifies the authority boundary. (4) Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false; Claude packet is read_only with promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false. (5) Hermes is control-plane only — CP855 Hermes packet has emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, and a command_matrix limited to validation commands. (6) production_ready is consistently false/pending and runtime_ready=false across manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, and adjudication, with runtime and enterprise trust explicitly deferred to human approval — no overclaim. Local marketplace validation passed (rp28:marketplace:validate and marketplace node:test both exit 0) and the readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Two P3 transparency notes: the full closeout validation suite and the finalization of adjudication/inspection/receipt remain pending (expected post-review sequencing), and the verified artifact is descriptor-only evidence rather than runtime API/UI.

findings.title: Full closeout validation suite and adjudication/inspection finalization still pending at review time
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only two executed commands (rp28_marketplace_validate_initial and marketplace_model_test_initial, both exit_code 0) with gate_outcome 'pending_after_initial_pack_evidence_creation' and hermes_gate.status 'pending'. The 10-command closeout suite named in the prompt (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) has not yet run, and construction-inspection.json verdict is PENDING_REVIEW while adjudication.md lists all severities as 'pending'. This is the expected pre-commit sequencing (evidence -> Claude review -> adjudication -> queue regeneration -> full validation -> commit), not a defect in the deliverable; this PASS is on descriptor coherence and boundaries, and pack closeout remains contingent on the full suite passing and on resolving the adjudication/inspection/receipt artifacts.
findings.recommendation: Before commit, run the complete validation suite plus git diff --check, then update construction-inspection.json (verdict/pack_production_ready) and adjudication.md from PENDING_REVIEW with this review's findings, and write the review-receipt.json referenced by the inspection.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; expected post-review closeout steps. Resolve by executing the full suite and finalizing adjudication/inspection/receipt prior to commit.

findings.title: Verified artifact is descriptor-only evidence, not runtime API/UI implementation
findings.details: manifest.json declares runtime_ready=false and implementation_layer='descriptor', and the 150 case-set rows (API fixture, Contract test, Build smoke, UI surface inventory, Primary interaction, etc.) are modeled as frozen descriptor/evidence stubs in registry.js with no executable API or UI runtime behind them. This matches the pack's declared scope (README line 25, MARKETPLACE_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, descriptor authority_boundary) and is therefore correct, but is flagged so the human approver does not read 'API/UI foundation' as delivered runtime surface. Runtime opening is routed to next_pack_id CP00-856 under human approval.
findings.recommendation: Keep the descriptor-only framing explicit in any closeout summary so the production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false posture is not misread as runtime API/UI completion; no change to the pack is required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; accurate reflection of declared descriptor-only scope. No remediation required.
